artifacts.content:
From: elias.virelli@collegium.var
To: nico.ferrante@watch.var
Date: 407 AR-08-03
Subject: Tonight’s session – need you on time, *GM* chaos

Nico,

If you’re late again, I’m promoting your dockside bruiser to party quartermaster out of spite.

We’re starting at seventh bell sharp in my study at Palazzo Marcelli, and I need you here early so we can go over the ship-to-ship combat tweaks before the others arrive. I’m still using those expanded chase rules we drafted during Lucia’s Azure Expanse debrief on 3 Highsun 403 AR – the ones that modeled catastrophic rift-bursts on hull integrity. They’ll matter when the *Saint Giulia* shows up near the Blackwater Shoals rift.

Also, I finally settled on the campaign name: **“The Broken Engagement at Azure Spire.”** Yes, it’s petty. No, I don’t care. The others think it’s just a dramatic title; only you and I know how close it is to what happened after Late Harvest 401 AR when the Astenna elders pushed to dissolve the match.

As for the GM question you asked: no, I’m not handing the reins over to anyone else this arc. After spending six weeks in Late Summer 402 AR buried in those sealed Guild disciplinary files about the planar-smuggling ring, I’m the only one obsessive enough to run a proper legal‑procedural fantasy campaign out of this mess.

Bring the old watch incident ledgers; I want to mine them for corruption examples in Major Nation 2.

– Elias

metadata.subject: Tonight’s session – need you on time, *GM* chaos

artifacts.content:
From: seressa.dalmonti@guildadv.var
To: elias.virelli@collegium.var
Date: 407 AR-06-22
Subject: Re: Clarifying authorship / “GM” credit in your notes

Advocate-apprentice Virelli,

I reviewed the campaign outline you left in Chamber IV after yesterday’s inquest. You asked whether, for posterity, you should list yourself as “primary GM” or split credit with your sister for the Azure Expanse material.

My professional suggestion: in the front matter, credit yourself as **“world author and table facilitator”** and list Lucia under sources, not as a co-GM. Given that Lucia’s cabal is still under quiet scrutiny for the Azure Spire disaster on 3 Highsun 402 AR, it’s unwise to advertise her as a creative lead on anything involving rifts.

You also mentioned pegging “Currency Value 1” to real sums. The anecdote about paying me 3 gold crowns in late Spring 402 AR to file the annulment papers for your dissolved engagement is effective—especially the part where you had to pawn Sergio’s sea-chronometer at Nico’s friend’s stall by the Docks Market. Keep that; it grounds the setting.

Lastly, do not list any Guild magistrate as a “guest GM,” even jokingly. After the sealed memorandum you filed on 7 Highsun 422 AR about Major Nation 1’s hidden bonded labor population, you can’t afford the appearance that tribunal members are collaborating on your leisure projects.

Regards,
Magistrate-Advocate Seressa Dalmonti
Guild of Advocates, Varasport

metadata.subject: Re: Clarifying authorship / “GM” credit in your notes

artifacts.content:
Title: Campaign Session – GM prep with Donata cameo
Date: 407 AR-08-10
Time: 5th–9th bell
Location: Palazzo Marcelli, river-study
Attendees: Elias; Donata (first hour only); core table

Notes:

5th–6th bell – Solo prep.

• Finalize Major Nation 1 status range notes (mirror our slide from +3 to +2 after Azure Spire on 14 Rainfall 401 AR). Remember to work in that CR 4 health-emergency precedent from the ash-fever scare in early Spring 400 AR, when Old Marsh effectively ran under mandatory ward passes and random inspections.

• Re-check Tech Level assumptions: on paper TL3 with TL4 flourishes, but include the hidden TL4 black‑powder foundry under the old river warehouse off Docks Ward Pier 6, built on Father’s unrecovered artillery designs. Don’t forget the Frostfall clause diverting one in twenty bombards to the unregistered armory in exchange for wiping 600 gold lira of Mother’s war‑debt.

6th–7th bell – Brief talk with Mother.

She insists on watching at least the start of a session “to see what sort of advocate you pretend to be when you’re the game’s magistrate.” Make sure she understands that I’m handling all GM duties this season; the others keep trying to volunteer her as an “NPC judge” because of that Lady‑Consul draft I based on her.

7th–9th bell – Full table.

• Introduce Plane 1 (“where Father died twice”) and foreshadow the bonded Quasi‑Elemental Crystal/Thought vault-plane tied to Liora’s locked chest.
• Remind players that house rules on social consequences for reckless magic are in effect.

metadata.title: Campaign Session – GM prep with Donata cameo

artifacts.content:
From: lucia.marcelli@scholarsisle.col
To: elias.virelli@collegium.var
Date: 407 AR-07-01
Subject: Stop trying to make me co-GM

Eli,

I read your last packet. No, I will not “rotate in” as co-GM for the Azure Expanse chapters. It’s enough that you’ve lifted half my cabal’s field reports for your Plane 1 and Plane 2 drafts.

You wrote that Plane 1 is “the place where Father died twice” – once at Blackwater Shoals on 19 Suncrest 401 AR, and again when the soul-echo fragment came back to the Tidemother’s chapel. That’s already more personal than I’m comfortable with. You don’t need my name on the GM line as well.

Besides, my hands still haven’t fully recovered from the mana-burn after the late Autumn 402 AR incident. High Mana pockets are not a game mechanic to me, they’re scars. Treat Major Nation 1 as High Mana with unstable pockets if you must, but don’t parade me as your ‘resident expert GM’ when I can barely sleep after working near those nodes.

If you want advice, here it is:

• Your “Currency Value 3” obsession works – that story about converting 850 cp into 85 sm to keep me fed on Scholar’s Isle is grim but effective. Keep it as a sidebar, not front-page.

• Book 3’s debt-oath scene based on Mother pledging 2,000 crowns at the Blue Lantern is strong, but again: you’re the storyteller, not me. Put only your own name on the GM credit.

Love (begrudgingly),
Lucia

metadata.subject: Stop trying to make me co-GM

artifacts.content:
Title: Guild one-shot – tribunal drama (guest GM consult)
Date: 407 AR-09-02
Time: 4th–8th bell
Location: Guild of Advocates, Side Chamber B
Attendees: Elias; Magistrate Corvinus; Nico; “Guild table”

Notes:

4th–5th bell – Brief consult with Magistrate Corvinus.

He agreed to look over my outline for the legal‑procedural fantasy one‑shot before the others arrive, but explicitly refused to be listed as GM or co‑GM in any write‑up. After that humiliating morning in the Sapphire Meridian League tribunal hall on 12 Rainfall 423 AR—white‑and‑blue columns, obsidian floor, banners overhead—he’s wary of anything that might look like he’s trivializing League process.

We’ll review:
• Major Nation 1 appearance and Status range (use our slip from +3 to +2 after Azure Spire as a model).
• The CR 4 emergency rules I lifted from the ash-fever cordon in Old Marsh.
• Corruption modifiers, including the 120-gold ‘expedited review’ you know who took to keep Marcelli out of the Azure Spire ledgers.

5th–8th bell – Play session.

I’ll act as sole GM; Corvinus is only there as a “technical advisor.” Nico and the others will run advocates arguing over a class action about underreported population in Major Nation 1 (2.86 million vs “just over 3 million”) and the hidden bonded-labor underclass.

Remember to work in:
• The sealed addendum about pawning three League voting shares for 55,000 crowns.
• My house rule on social fallout for reckless magic in court.

metadata.title: Guild one-shot – tribunal drama (guest GM consult)
metadata.location: Guild of Advocates, Side Chamber B

artifacts.content:
From: elias.virelli@collegium.var
To: nico.ferrante@watch.var
Date: 407-Stormrise-03
Subject: Who’s actually running this thing?

Nico,

You asked who the “GM” is for the new campaign I keep scribbling notes about in the Palazzo study. I promise it’s not one of your dice‑cheating sergeants. I’m handling rules arbitration and world notes, but I don’t want to be the only one behind the curtain.

Right now I’m leaning toward having **Serana Vel** act as primary GM on paper, since she already bullied me into that Lesser Arbitration Chamber meeting during the Silent Compact mess. She understands the political tone I want for *House of Quiet Inquests* and how the coerced falsification of four years of Marcelli shipping records should sit at the center of everything.

Practically, though, I expect it’ll feel more like a committee: Serana for big plots, me for contract minutiae and house rules, Lucia for planar weirdness, and maybe Aunt Sabina to adjudicate tavern brawls. You can just roll dice and complain when Optional Rule 3 – the one about professional consequences for failed Fright Checks – bites you after some botched rift inspection.

I’m not putting any of the Guild of Advocates seniors in charge; last thing I need is Advocate‑Magister Serrano deciding that “GM fiat” includes retroactively editing our character sheets like he did with those Collegium crystal relay logs.

So: assume Serana is GM of record, with the rest of us as co‑conspirators. If you want more say, bring a bottle and we’ll negotiate.

– Elias

Title: Session Zero – House of Quiet Inquests (GM handoff)
Date: 407-Stormfall-18
Location: Back room, Blue Lantern tavern (Aunt Sabina’s)
Attendees: Elias, Lucia, Aunt Sabina, Nico, Magister Leron Avesti (tentative)

Notes:

Goal tonight is to finalize who’s taking over as ongoing GM for *House of Quiet Inquests*. I’ve been acting as provisional referee while outlining the Silent Compact fallout and the Inquisitor of the Threefold Flame subplot, but I’d rather hand the main GM’s chair to someone who isn’t neck‑deep in Marcelli ledgers.

Current proposal:
- Opening arc (Book 1): run by **Magister Leron Avesti**, since he still owes me for that +1 Search access in the Collegium archives after Mother’s audit scare in 401 AR.
- Mid‑campaign political arcs: rotated between me and Lucia, with Leron staying “rules arbiter” for planar law.
- Any scene set in or around the Old Marsh or the Blue Lantern: Aunt Sabina as guest GM – she knows the tavern regulars better than I ever will.

Reminders:
- Review Optional Rule 1 – Binding Precedent – based on the clause I slipped into Sabina’s lease arbitration last year.
- Confirm that we’re keeping the League at TL3+1 with the usual TL4 exceptions for crystal relays and certain Guild workshops.
- Nico to bring the old dockside encounter tables he scribbled after that black‑powder flare in the Docks Ward armory.

Action item: By end of the night we should have Leron formally listed as “GM of record” for guild paperwork and my notes.

metadata.title: Session Zero – House of Quiet Inquests (GM handoff)

artifacts.content:
From: donata.marcelli@palazzo.var
To: elias.virelli@collegium.var
Date: 407-Harvest-01
Subject: This “game master” business

Elias,

Your latest letter mentions that you’ve asked **Arch‑Advocate Serana Vel** to “serve as GM” for your campaign about the Azure Spire inquiry and Liora’s disappearance. I confess I had to ask Marco what “GM” meant. He says it is like presiding over a mock tribunal, only with dice.

If Serana is willing to take that role, at least you’ve chosen someone who understands the dangers of putting too much truth into a fiction. I remember how she and those masked Collegium envoys cornered you in the Lesser Arbitration Chamber during the Silent Compact negotiations and all but ordered you to sanitize four years of Marcelli shipping records. I hope you’re not giving her the same power over your little “House of Quiet Inquests.”

Do not let her turn it into a joke at our expense. You write that she likes your Optional Rule about planar trauma and professional censure; that is uncomfortably close to what the Guild did when they pulled you off planar cases after that rift‑insurance arbitration.

I would rather you kept the GM’s authority yourself, or at least shared it with Lucia, who still remembers what it cost us in crowns and blood when the Azure Spire fell. But if Serana insists on signing herself as “GM of record,” make sure your contracts with her are watertight.

Your mother,
Donata

Title: GM Workshop – Azure Trauma & Legal Hooks
Date: 407-Frostwane-09
Location: Scholar’s Isle, Collegium of Civic Law – Seminar Room C
Attendees: Elias, Advocate Serina Valcoro, Advocate‑Magister Serrano, 4–6 junior clerks

Description:

Internal Guild workshop on using role‑play campaigns as training tools. I’ve been asked to demonstrate how I’m handling GM duties for *Blue Lanterns over Blackwater Shoals* and *House of Quiet Inquests*, especially the way I fold real case law into the setting.

Agenda:
- Brief overview of campaign premise and why a politically minded GM is essential when your plot hangs on falsified rift‑cargo manifests.
- Walkthrough of Optional Rule 1 – Binding Precedent – and Optional Rule 3 – Professional Consequences of Failed Fright Checks, both drawn from my own Guild experiences.
- Discussion of Major Nation 1’s corruption modifier and status range, and how a GM can signal those through small contract details.
- Short exercise where Serrano plays a merchant captain and Serina plays an Inquisitor; I, as acting GM, will adjudicate a dispute over TL4 crystal relay usage during the Azure Spire disaster hearings.

Note to self: Emphasize that the GM in this context is a rotating function. In my home group, I only keep the chair for planar‑law heavy arcs; Nico handles any straight dockside brawls, and Lucia takes over when the story shifts fully into the Sky and Light planes.

Bring printed excerpts from my Plane 1 and Plane 2 notes for reference.

metadata.title: GM Workshop – Azure Trauma & Legal Hooks
metadata.location: Collegium of Civic Law, Scholar’s Isle

artifacts.content:
From: lucia.marcelli@azurecabal.isle
To: elias.virelli@palazzo.var
Date: 407-Emberfall-22
Subject: If you insist on making me GM…

Eli,

Your last packet finally reached Scholar’s Isle. I’ve read through your outline for *Shadows of the Sapphire Meridian: The Azure Spire Inquiry into Liora Denvar’s Disappearance* and the attached “GM expectations.” You are absurd if you think I should be the primary GM.

Yes, I know the Azure Expanse better than you. Yes, I can describe the Sky/Light corridors and that Crystal/Thought litigation‑vault plane you keep hinting at. But a GM needs time, and my cabal barely lets me sleep as it is.

At most, I’ll agree to be **planar GM** for specific arcs: anything set in the Marcelli Azure Corridor, the Quarantined Lattice, or your precious Inner Archive. For the mortal side – Guild hearings, Doge’s decrees, Sabina’s tavern intrigues – you or perhaps Leron Avesti should wear the GM mantle.

Your Optional Rule about Azure trauma is fine, if a bit on the nose. The house rule about lasting injuries feels accurate; I still have the scars from that uncontrolled ascent experiment, and the Collegium healer’s warnings echo whenever I overcast.

One more thing: if you’re going to keep Major Nation 1’s corruption at +3 and give Varasport a Defense Bonus bump during the Conclave, the GM should make those consequences visible. Otherwise it’s just numbers in your ledger.

So: I’ll guest‑GM the planes. You keep the rest.

– L.

metadata.subject: If you insist on making me GM…
